quote: That which does not kill us makes us stronger.
God is dead. And we have killed him.
Become who you are.
Live dangerously.
He who fights with monsters should look to it that he himself does not become a monster. And if you gaze long into an abyss, the abyss also gazes into you.
Love is blind; friendship closes its eyes.
There are no facts, only interpretations.
There are no moral phenomena at all, only a moral interpretation of phenomena.
One must still have chaos in oneself to be able to give birth to a dancing star.
He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
Convictions are more dangerous enemies of truth than lies.
In solitude the lonely person is eaten up by himself, among crowds by the many.
I would only believe in a god who could dance.
My memory says, 'I did that.' My pride replies, 'I could not have done that.' Eventually, memory yields.
The will to power.
He who cannot give anything away cannot feel anything either.
The thought of death gives me great happiness.
Man is something that shall be overcome.
We have art in order not to die of the truth.
The individual has always had to struggle to keep from being overwhelmed by the tribe.
